academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need an upper secondary school diploma or equivalent foreign qualification with at least 12 years of schooling and must pass the programme's restricted-access selection.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Upper secondary school diploma (or equivalent) with at least 12 years of schooling, suitable for access to higher education, to be obtained by July 31, 2026.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Selection is based on the CEnT-S test sections in mathematics, reasoning on texts and data, physics, chemistry, and biology, followed by an online interview focused on animal biology.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: No GPA threshold is stated. Applicants with foreign qualifications must also satisfy the University of Padua country-specific entry-title requirements.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 (CEFR) or equivalent, to be achieved by July 31, 2026.
accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training with minimum score 6.0
Cambridge English B2 First with minimum score 173
Oxford Test of English with minimum score 126
Pearson PTE Academic with minimum score 65
Other certificates listed on the official University of Padua language requirement page for English-taught programmes
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if their previous education used as the entry title was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua on the official language requirement page.

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call two is a second selection only if places remain unfilled after the first ranking. This call applies to EU and EU-equated candidates with a foreign qualification; non-EU candidates residing in Italy also follow the EU/EU-equated route when applicable.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: This call is for non-EU candidates residing abroad. Application submission closes at 11:59 pm local time (GMT+1) on March 7, 2026.

required_documents.notes: EU and EU-equated candidates may use a valid identity document or passport; non-EU abroad candidates are asked for a valid passport.

required_documents.document_name: Proof of CEnT-S registration and test results
required_documents.notes: Upload proof of registration plus provisional and normalised CEnT-S results; if not yet available at application stage, upload them as soon as released in the CISIA reserved area.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records and upper secondary school diploma
required_documents.notes: Upload the transcript and, if already obtained, the upper secondary diploma with at least 12 years of schooling.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required if educational documents are issued in a language other than Italian or English.
Upload if already obtained; otherwise it must be achieved and submitted by July 31, 2026. Exemption documentation can be used where allowed by the university language rules.
Required as part of the online application.

required_documents.document_name: Legalised diploma or Apostille or CIMEA Statement of Verification
required_documents.notes: By October 31, 2026 admitted candidates must submit one of these forms of diploma verification. A CIMEA Statement of Comparability may also be requested if needed.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: CEnT-S by CISIA plus online interview
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants; the same selection model is used for EU/EU-equated and non-EU abroad calls, with different application windows.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Valid CEnT-S dates: 27 November 2025, 16 December 2025, 15 January 2026 (resit 20 January 2026), 19 February 2026, 26 February 2026, 12 March 2026 (resit 19 March 2026). The interview schedule is communicated by email to eligible candidates.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Candidates must obtain a normalised weighted CEnT-S score of at least 20 to access the interview and at least 30/120 in the online interview. Ranking is based on the sum of the CEnT-S and interview scores.

uncertainty_notes: The University of Padua states that English translations of admission documents are provided for information; where disputes arise, the Italian version has legal validity.
